name: DOGE 4h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: price recovers short-term value after a flush, confirmation improves across bars, and exposure is built in stages instead of one-shot timing
thesis: Cycle 79 explicitly asks for scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing. DOGE 4h is the best-fit lane because the regime is live transition and should produce more flush-and-recovery sequences than TAO trend-up or ETH pure chop. The edge is adding only as recovery proves itself, not guessing the first bounce.
invalidation_condition: If DOGE 4h value-recovery sequences remain too sparse or if staged adds mostly average into failed bounces, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes away from value, reclaims value, confirms that reclaim across successive bars, and resumes away from the recovery zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition markets often punish one-shot timing but reward building into improving confirmation. Staged entry after recovery should improve location compared with full-size first-touch entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best fit because it is the cycle primary lane, in transition, and active enough to print repeated recovery sequences without forcing 1h noise back into the process.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The staged entries may still build into weak mean-reversion bounces that fail before real continuation appears.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is good but expectancy is weak, reduce second-add size and take the first partial sooner before changing the trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated recovery attempts, many modest first-target wins, and fewer larger moves when the recovery becomes true continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h clears density and staged entries materially outperform one-shot recovery timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated scale-ins that turn into averaging losers.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because flush-and-recovery events recur in transition

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit transition lane for staged value recovery.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h shows enough value-recovery sequences with scale-in improving net expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h staged entries mostly build into failed bounces.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with several recovery continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same scale-in recovery survives on a flatter similar lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h retains a smaller but tradable staged recovery behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h becomes too flat or too shallow to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, smaller recovery continuations than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same staged recovery logic survives a stronger trend-contaminated lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h retains enough recovery structure without turning into late continuation buying.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the concept is too chop/transition specific.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger recovered continuation than DOGE.

long: Initial 60% size on first close back above VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes >= EMA_20 and low stays >= VWAP - 0.08 * ATR_14
short: Initial 60% size on first close back below VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes <= EMA_20 and high stays <= VWAP + 0.08 * ATR_14

long: Stop loss: 0.84 * ATR_14 below blended entry
short: Stop loss: 0.84 * ATR_14 above blended entry
